questions.question: Who was the founder of the Mauryan dynasty?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta Maurya was the founder of the Mauryan dynasty with the help of Chanakya.

questions.question: The capital of the Vijayanagara Empire was
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara was the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire.

questions.question: The Indian National Congress was founded in
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885 by A.O. Hume.

questions.question: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in
questions.explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1920.

questions.question: The ancient Indian university of Takshashila was located in
questions.explanation: The ancient Indian university of Takshashila was located in present-day Pakistan.

questions.question: The Delhi Sultanate was founded by
questions.explanation: The Delhi Sultanate was founded by Qutb-ud-din Aibak.

questions.question: The Permanent Settlement of 1793 was introduced by
questions.explanation: The Permanent Settlement of 1793 was introduced by Lord Cornwallis.

questions.question: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1942.

questions.question: The Mughal emperor who built the Taj Mahal was
questions.explanation: The Mughal emperor who built the Taj Mahal was Shah Jahan.

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilization was discovered in
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley Civilization was discovered in the 1920s by Daya Ram Sahni.

questions.question: What is the largest river island in the Indian subcontinent?
questions.explanation: Majuli is the largest river island in the Indian subcontinent, located in the Brahmaputra River.

questions.question: Which mountain range runs along the border between India and Myanmar?
questions.explanation: The Purvanchal mountain range runs along the border between India and Myanmar.

questions.question: What is the name of the strait that separates the islands of Little Andaman and Great Nicobar?
questions.explanation: The Ten Degree Channel is the strait that separates the islands of Little Andaman and Great Nicobar.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers flows through the Satpura range?
questions.explanation: The Narmada river flows through the Satpura range.

questions.question: What is the name of the plateau that covers most of Maharashtra, Gujarat, and parts of Madhya Pradesh?
questions.explanation: The Deccan Plateau covers most of Maharashtra, Gujarat, and parts of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.question: Which state has the longest coastline in India?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has the longest coastline in India.

questions.question: What is the name of the highest peak in the Eastern Ghats?
questions.explanation: Arma Konda is the highest peak in the Eastern Ghats.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is known for its backward flow during monsoons?
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra river is known for its backward flow during monsoons.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest delta in India?
questions.explanation: The Ganga-Brahmaputra Delta is the largest delta in India.

questions.question: Which of the following states has the highest number of major ports?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has the highest number of major ports.

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with the Fundamental Rights of citizens?
questions.explanation: Part III of the Indian Constitution deals with the Fundamental Rights of citizens.

questions.question: What is the minimum age for a person to become a member of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: According to Article 84 of the Indian Constitution, a person must be at least 25 years old to become a member of the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the independence of the judiciary?
questions.explanation: Article 50 of the Indian Constitution provides for the separation of judiciary from the executive, ensuring the independence of the judiciary.

questions.question: What is the name of the local self-government system at the village level in India?
questions.explanation: Panchayati Raj is the local self-government system at the village level in India, established under the 73rd Amendment Act of 1992.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a fundamental right guaranteed by the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Right to Property was removed as a fundamental right by the 44th Amendment Act of 1978 and is now a constitutional right under Article 300A.

questions.question: Who is the head of the Indian Judiciary?
questions.explanation: The Chief Justice of India is the head of the Indian Judiciary, responsible for overseeing the administration of justice in the country.

questions.question: What is the term of office for a member of the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: The term of office for a member of the Rajya Sabha is 6 years, with one-third of the members retiring every 2 years.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the right to freedom of speech and expression?
questions.explanation: Article 19 of the Indian Constitution provides for the right to freedom of speech and expression, subject to reasonable restrictions.

questions.question: Who has the power to appoint the Judges of the Supreme Court of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India has the power to appoint the Judges of the Supreme Court of India, in consultation with the Chief Justice of India and other senior judges.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 73rd and 74th Amendment Acts to the Indian Constitution?
options: To establish a federal system of government
To provide for the independence of the judiciary
To guarantee the fundamental rights of citizens
To provide for decentralization of power and local self-government
questions.explanation: The 73rd and 74th Amendment Acts to the Indian Constitution aimed to provide for decentralization of power and local self-government, by establishing Panchayati Raj institutions at the village level and municipalities in urban areas.
